subject: Databases
title: Saga Pattern
description: Learn Saga Pattern in one minute.

hook.text: A booking spans three services: payment, inventory, notification. No single database transaction can cover all three.

buildup.text: A saga is a sequence of local transactions. Each step has a compensating action that undoes it if a later step fails.

discovery.text: Step 1: charge payment. Step 2: reserve inventory. If step 2 fails, run compensator: refund payment.

twist.text: Sagas provide eventual consistency, not ACID. Between steps, the system is temporarily inconsistent.

climax.text: Orchestrated sagas use a central coordinator. Choreographed sagas let each service react to events.

punchline.text: Sagas: distributed transactions via compensating actions. Eventual, not atomic.

quiz.question: How do sagas handle failures in distributed systems?
options: By running compensating actions that undo the effects of previously completed steps
By rolling back all databases at once
By ignoring the failure and continuing with the next step
